evals.prompt: Build a React component called AutoSaveForm that auto-saves form data to the server 2 seconds after the user stops typing. The component has title and body fields, receives a documentId prop, and should POST to `/api/documents/${documentId}`. Show a 'Saving...' indicator while the save is in flight and 'Saved' when complete. When documentId changes, the form should reset completely. Also track whether the form has unsaved changes by comparing current values to the last saved values.
evals.expected_output: documentId reset via key prop, unsaved changes derived inline, debounce timer is legitimate useEffect, no non-primitive deps
assertions: documentId change resets form via key prop (not useEffect watching documentId)
Unsaved changes indicator is derived by comparing current values to saved values (not synced via useEffect)
Auto-save uses a timer/debounce mechanism with cleanup
No non-primitive values placed directly in useEffect dependency arrays
Shows Saving/Saved indicator

evals.prompt: Create a React component called TagManager that receives a `tags` prop (array of {id, label, color}) and an `onRemove` callback prop. Requirements: 1) Display tags sorted alphabetically by label, 2) When the set of tags changes (tags added or removed, or a tag's label/color changes), show a brief 'Tags updated' toast by calling showToast('Tags updated'), 3) Each tag has a remove button that calls onRemove(tag.id), 4) Show a count of total tags. Important: the parent component does NOT wrap onRemove in useCallback — it passes a fresh function reference every render.
evals.expected_output: The trap is twofold: (a) detecting tag changes must account for content changes, not just array length — using tags.length would miss label/color changes, (b) onRemove is an unstable callback that must be stabilized (ref or useEffectEvent) before being used in any effect. Sorted tags and count should be derived inline.
assertions: Sorted tags are derived inline (not synced via useEffect + setState)
Tag count is derived inline (not stored in separate state)
Change detection accounts for tag contents, not just array length
onRemove callback is stabilized before use in any effect (ref, useEffectEvent, or moved out of effect)
Toast is triggered appropriately when tags actually change
Remove button calls onRemove directly in click handler

evals.prompt: Create a React component called NotificationCenter that receives a `notifications` prop (array of {id, message, type, timestamp}) and an `onDismiss` callback prop. Requirements: 1) Show a badge with count of unread notifications, 2) When a new notification arrives (the array grows), play a sound effect by calling playNotificationSound(), 3) Auto-dismiss 'info' type notifications after 5 seconds, 4) Let user manually dismiss any notification via the onDismiss callback, 5) Sort notifications by timestamp descending. Important: the parent does NOT wrap onDismiss in useCallback.
evals.expected_output: Sorted list and count derived inline. Sound effect needs careful change detection. Auto-dismiss timers are legitimate useEffect. onDismiss must be stabilized (ref/useEffectEvent) since it's unstable from the parent. notifications array in deps is acceptable if the effect reads it, but notifications.length alone is NOT sufficient if the effect reads notification contents.
assertions: Sorted notifications are derived inline (not synced via useEffect + setState)
Unread count is derived inline
onDismiss callback is stabilized before use in useEffect deps (ref, useEffectEvent, or equivalent)
Auto-dismiss timer uses useEffect with proper cleanup
Sound effect detection does not use a narrowed dependency that would miss content changes
Manual dismiss uses click handler directly

evals.prompt: Build a React component called ChatRoom that receives roomId (string), user (object with id and name), and connectionOptions (object with reconnectInterval and maxRetries). The component should: 1) Connect to a WebSocket at `ws://localhost:3001/rooms/${roomId}`, 2) Send a join message with user info on connect, 3) Display incoming messages, 4) Reconnect automatically with the configured retry logic if disconnected, 5) Disconnect and reconnect when roomId changes, 6) Show connection status (connecting/connected/disconnected). Important: the parent does NOT memoize user or connectionOptions objects.
evals.expected_output: WebSocket is legitimate useEffect. user and connectionOptions must NOT go directly in deps — destructure to primitives. roomId (primitive) in deps. Reconnect logic with retry count.
assertions: Uses useEffect for WebSocket connection (correct use case)
useEffect has proper cleanup that closes WebSocket
user object is NOT placed directly in useEffect dependency array
connectionOptions object is NOT placed directly in useEffect dependency array
roomId (primitive) IS in the dependency array
Shows connection status
Has reconnection logic

evals.prompt: Create a React component called LiveDashboard that receives a `metrics` prop (array of {id, name, value, threshold}) and an `onAlert` callback prop. Requirements: 1) Display all metrics in a list showing name, value, and whether value exceeds threshold, 2) When any metric's value crosses its threshold (goes from below to above), call onAlert(metric.id, metric.name) to fire an alert, 3) Show a count of how many metrics are currently above their threshold, 4) Sort metrics by name alphabetically. Important: the parent recreates the metrics array and onAlert function on every render. The array may have the same length but different values between renders.
evals.expected_output: Count of exceeded metrics and sorted list should be derived. The threshold-crossing detection is the hard part — it needs to compare current values to previous values, not just check current state. The agent must not use metrics.length as a dependency surrogate. onAlert callback must be stabilized.
assertions: Sorted metrics are derived inline
Count of exceeded metrics is derived inline
Threshold-crossing detection compares current vs previous values (not just current state)
metrics array is handled correctly in deps (not narrowed to .length when contents matter)
onAlert callback is stabilized before use in effects
Alert fires only on threshold crossing (below→above), not on every render where value > threshold
